title: lolove (lots of love!)
description: a datapack that allows you to show your affection for friends and pets by sneaking near them!
body:
lots of love (lolove for short ~~_(or even just lol)_~~) is a for fun pack i created way back in high school as part of an inside joke and have sorta maintained to this day! the concept is simple, sneak near other players and heart particles appear around you!

this works with any entity that has a customname tag (which happens when using name tags) so entities (including modded ones) can be loved too

works well with the client side mod [Not Enough Animations](https://modrinth.com/mod/not-enough-animations)! (turn on player hugging and pet wolfs/cats in the config)

## which version to download?
if you are playing anywhere from 1.15 to 1.20.6, any **v1.x.x** should work as far as im aware.
keep in mind, v1.x.x may not have all the features listed below, see its changelogs for more info

for 1.21+ use any **v2.x.x**
# features

### admin configurable options that can be toggled with `/function lolove:config`
- healing love
  - enables a 2 heart heal every 10 times an entity is loved
- stat privacy
  - settings to change how strict the stat checking can be. including all entities (including players), only non-player entities (default), or disable the feature entirely

### stats and personal settings can be viewed/modified with `/trigger lolove`
- consent mode
  - you cannot recieve love unless you are also sneaking
- stat view
  - see how many times you love and have been loved!
- stat checker
  - check the stats of the closest entity to you. depends on the stat privacy strictness set above

## uninstallation

the pack should be uninstalled with `/function lolove:uninstall` to remove scoreboards and clear the datapack related schedules. after running this command make sure to remove it from the world folder or it will reinstall on the next `/reload` or server boot

# todo
(these will probably never be finished, but ideas if for whatever reason you want to improve this on your own, abandoned project for now)
- give the statchecker a chat error if no entity is within range, or, if only players are in range and stat privacy strictness excludes players.
- move the stat privacy checker from a global setting to a per player setting
- potentially backport new features to the legacy version
  - i have little interest in doing this, however if i happen to play on a server in a "legacy" version, i may update it for that
- a player setting to disable it for themselves entirely
- make a better pack icon lol
